summary: CONFIRMED, still present. The Inherit/Set/Clear tri-state for LLM-identity provider_params and auth_binding is hand-rolled as a (Option<T>, clear_*: bool) split across 7 seam structs (the audit named 5; two more exist: meerkat-runtime SessionLlmReconfigureRequest and the RPC-local TurnOverrides). The participating fields are exactly provider_params and auth_binding (model/provider have no clear flag). Meaning is re-derived via "if clear { None } else { x.or(current) }" at 3 runtime sites (session.rs:3818/3849, session_recovery.rs:487/547, meerkat/llm_reconfigure.rs:300/335) and the illegal set+clear fourth state forces hand-maintained reject branches in two runtime resolvers (SessionLlmIdentityOverrideError::SetAndClear* session.rs:3764-3767; the llm_reconfigure ValidationFailed pair :272-281) AND in the generated SessionDocumentMachine (ResumeOverrideRejection::ClearAndSet{ProviderParams,AuthBinding}) emitted to BOTH meerkat-core/src/generated/session_document.rs (used at runtime by session_recovery) and meerkat-machine-kernels/src/generated/session_document.rs, from one DSL spec at meerkat-machine-schema/src/catalog/dsl/session_document.rs. The canonical typed owner TurnMetadataOverride<T>{Set,Clear} exists (run_primitive.rs:1260) and is correctly used by RuntimeTurnMetadata and the wire WireTurnMetadataOverride<T> with full legacy-clear serde-boundary deserialization (the exact gold-standard precedent to copy). The RPC layer literally holds both representations and translates Option+clear<->TurnMetadataOverride at session_runtime.rs:1479-1573, proving the typed form already round-trips.

summary: The violation is still present. `SessionMetadata.realm_id` is `Option<String>` (meerkat-core/src/session.rs:3693), durably JSON-serialized into the session metadata map under SESSION_METADATA_KEY (set_session_metadata/try_session_metadata, session.rs:3041/3066). It is one node in a wide family of untyped `Option<String>` realm carriers (`AgentBuildConfig.realm_id` factory.rs:409, `SessionBuildOptions.realm_id` service/mod.rs:265, `RealmDefaults.realm_id` + `SurfaceSessionRecoveryContext.realm_id` session_recovery.rs:67/117, plus config-store siblings `ConfigStoreMetadata.realm_id` config_store.rs:26, `ConfigEnvelope.realm_id` config_runtime.rs:35, `RuntimeHostMetadataProjection.realm_id` surface.rs:113) that all re-derive into a typed `RealmId` decision via `RealmId::parse` at the factory resolver hop (meerkat/src/factory.rs:1450/1476/2839). The runtime layer is ALREADY typed (SessionRuntime holds `RwLock<Option<RealmId>>`, session_runtime/mod.rs:110, meerkat-rpc/src/session_runtime.rs:1307) but is deliberately downgraded back to String to populate the build options (live_orchestration.rs:549-551 `self.realm_id.map(ToString::to_string)`; RPC main.rs:231 `locator.realm.as_str().to_string()`). realm_id is NOT a strict duplicate of auth_binding.realm: in `resolve_auth_binding_or_default_for_provider` (connection.rs:437-476) auth_binding.realm is canonical when a binding is present, and realm_id is only the fallback preferred-realm hint when auth_binding is None; both are persisted independently in SessionMetadata. The correct repair is typing the field(s) `Option<RealmId>` (parse-once at serde boundary) — NOT removal, since the fallback semantic is real.

typed_owner.notes: RealmId is a slug_newtype with #[serde(try_from = "String", into = "String")], so Option<RealmId> serializes to the EXACT SAME JSON string as Option<String> — zero wire-shape change, only validation-at-deserialize. Two in-repo precedents already adopt exactly this repair: meerkat-contracts/src/session_locator.rs:14 (realm_id: Option<RealmId>) and meerkat-store/src/realm.rs:663 (RealmManifest lifts persisted slug to typed RealmId at the deserialize boundary). typed_owner.notes: ModelId(String) is the canonical model-identity newtype and is ALREADY adopted by RuntimeTurnMetadata.model: Option<ModelId> (run_primitive.rs:1362). The minimal Rule-4 repair is to retype the surface input fields from Option<String> to Option<ModelId> so model identity is typed end-to-end, exactly as RPC already does at session_runtime.rs:1514 (.map(ModelId::new)). Do NOT adopt TurnMetadataOverride<ModelId> for model: model is non-clearable (no clear_model path exists; a session always has an effective model), so it is genuinely binary (None=inherit/preserve vs Some=set), unlike provider_params/auth_binding which legitimately use TurnMetadataOverride. ResumeOverrideMask.model: bool is the typed explicit-override-intent fact and is the CORRECT representation of override presence — it already discharges the 'which model wins' decision at factory.rs:3466; it should remain a bool (it is not a value-carrying field). The generated machine input model_override_present: bool is likewise a legitimate presence fact and must not change.

repair_steps: SCOPE NOTE: The audit's '3 meanings (clear)' premise is wrong — model is binary, not tri-state — so the TurnMetadataOverride<T> path (H1) does NOT apply to model. summary: The M9 violation is still present in meerkat-mob/src/runtime/flow.rs. `effective_step_timeout` (now at line 2537) returns `Result<(Duration, Option<String>), MobError>` where the second tuple element is a single `Option<String>` that simultaneously encodes (a) the lifecycle decision — Some => the step deadline was clamped by the flow deadline so on timeout the whole flow must fail terminally with no retry; None => an ordinary step timeout that may be retried — and (b) the human-readable display reason. That `Option<String>` is named `flow_deadline_timeout_reason` and threaded through both dispatch paths (single-target at line 552-573, multi-target at line 625-663) into `execute_single_target_with_retries` (param at line 744), where the `if let Some(reason) = flow_deadline_timeout_reason` branch at line 896 makes the terminal-vs-retryable decision. The lifecycle meaning is re-derived from Option presence at the consumer, not carried by a type. The proper fix is a typed enum (e.g. `StepTimeoutOutcome { Retry, FailFlow { reason: String } }`) returned by `effective_step_timeout` and threaded through to the consumer. The scope is entirely internal to one runtime file: no wire types, no contracts, no generated machine, no durable metadata, and no Rust tests directly exercise `effective_step_timeout`.

summary: The violation still exists. MobMcpState in meerkat-mob-mcp/src/lib.rs models realm-profile-store provenance as a tri-state pair: `realm_profile_store: Option<Arc<dyn RealmProfileStore>>` (line 221) plus a parallel `realm_profile_store_explicit: bool` (line 227). The bool encodes WHY the store has its current value (caller-supplied vs default/auto-upgradeable) — a semantic distinction carried by an untyped bool that is re-derived into a decision at line 283 (`if !self.realm_profile_store_explicit { ...auto-upgrade default to SQLite... }`). This is entirely builder-local: the flag is never serialized, never persisted, and never crosses any wire or surface boundary. It is set false in the default constructor (line 258), set true in `with_realm_profile_store` (line 269), and read only at the persistent-root guard (line 283). The store itself flows onward via `bind_realm_profile_store` (line 424) and `require_realm_profile_store` (line 1787) — but those consume only the resolved store, not the provenance flag. A `StoreSelection` enum collapses the two fields into one typed source of truth.

typed_owner.notes: No existing typed owner. Design a small builder-local enum that fuses the Option<store> + explicit:bool pair into one source of truth. Suggested shape: `enum RealmProfileStoreSelection { /* default, may be auto-upgraded to SQLite under a persistent root */ DefaultInMemory(Arc<dyn RealmProfileStore>), /* upgraded from default to durable SQLite */ DefaultDurable(Arc<dyn RealmProfileStore>), /* caller-supplied, must never be overridden */ CallerSupplied(Option<Arc<dyn RealmProfileStore>>) }`. The persistent-root upgrade guard becomes `matches!(self.realm_profile_store_selection, RealmProfileStoreSelection::DefaultInMemory(_))` instead of `!self.realm_profile_store_explicit`. A `fn store(&self) -> Option<&Arc<dyn RealmProfileStore>>` accessor preserves the existing `realm_profile_store()` getter contract. RealmProfileStore itself already exists at meerkat-mob/src/store/realm_profile.rs:29 (re-exported via meerkat_mob).

repair_steps: 1. In meerkat-mob-mcp/src/lib.rs, define a private builder-local enum just above `pub struct MobMcpState` (~line 208): `enum RealmProfileStoreSelection { DefaultInMemory(Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>), DefaultDurable(Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>), CallerSupplied(Option<Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>>) }`. Add an accessor `impl RealmProfileStoreSelection { fn store(&self) -> Option<&Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>> { match self { Self::DefaultInMemory(s) | Self::DefaultDurable(s) => Some(s), Self::CallerSupplied(opt) => opt.as_ref() } } fn is_default(&self) -> bool { matches!(self, Self::DefaultInMemory(_)) } }`.

2. Replace the two struct fields at lines 221 and 227 with a single field: delete `realm_profile_store: Option<Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>>,` and `realm_profile_store_explicit: bool,` (plus the doc comments lines 222-226) and add `realm_profile_store_selection: RealmProfileStoreSelection,` with a short doc explaining it is the single source of truth for store provenance.

3. In `new_with_runtime_adapter` (lines 257-258), replace the two initializer lines with `realm_profile_store_selection: RealmProfileStoreSelection::DefaultInMemory(Arc::new(meerkat_mob::InMemoryRealmProfileStore::new())),`.

5. In `realm_profile_store()` getter (lines 274-276), replace `self.realm_profile_store.as_ref()` with `self.realm_profile_store_selection.store()`.

6. In `with_persistent_storage_root` (line 283), replace the guard `if !self.realm_profile_store_explicit {` with `if self.realm_profile_store_selection.is_default() {`. On successful SQLite open (line 287), set `self.realm_profile_store_selection = RealmProfileStoreSelection::DefaultDurable(Arc::new(store) as Arc<dyn meerkat_mob::RealmProfileStore>);` instead of mutating `self.realm_profile_store`.
7. In `bind_realm_profile_store` (line 425), replace `self.realm_profile_store.clone()` with `self.realm_profile_store_selection.store().cloned()`.
8. In `require_realm_profile_store` (line 1787), replace `self.realm_profile_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(...)` with `self.realm_profile_store_selection.store().ok_or_else(...)`.
